topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The spreadsheet glowed under the harsh office lights, illuminating the stark reality. I traced the numbers again, my pen hovering over the figures detailing project allocations. Then, the realization hit me like a physical blow: Sarah, the new intern, was earning nearly double what I was, despite having barely started. A tight knot formed in my stomach, and I instinctively pulled my cardigan tighter around myself. Every glance I caught from my supervisor felt like a spotlight, exposing this sudden, unwelcome disparity. I quickly minimized the window, pretending to be deeply engrossed in a distant email, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to regain composure.

[story 2]

It happened during the monthly budget review. I was meticulously reviewing departmental spending, feeling the familiar prickle of inadequacy as I compared my salary band to the figures presented. When the projection for the junior analyst, Mark, was read aloud—a number significantly higher than mine—a cold wash spread through my chest. I focused intently on my notepad, my jaw tightening so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I kept my eyes fixed on the glossy paper, avoiding any direct contact with Mark, whose quiet confidence seemed to radiate outward, leaving me feeling small and suddenly very visible in the sterile conference room.

[story 3]

I overheard the casual conversation near the water cooler. "Wow, Sarah's bonus this quarter was huge," someone remarked. The words landed in my ears with unwelcome precision. My hands, which had been resting loosely on the desk, clenched into fists under the veneer of calm. I shifted my weight from one foot to the other, a nervous habit I couldn't seem to shake. I forced a bright, brittle smile when my manager approached, my voice coming out a little too high-pitched. I desperately wanted the floor to swallow me whole, wishing the polished linoleum beneath my shoes would just disappear.
